author: Lilian
aspect_detail: Udon texture was a bit too dense and not bouncy
original_text: A fun udon spot to try out, cafeteria style! Udon is made fresh on onsite. I got the beef udon with 2 shrimp tempura, it was good but not worth the 1 hour 10 mins on Saturday night at 7pm. Udon texture was a bit too dense and not bouncy. The beef on the udon was delicious though! Shrimp tempura was crispy and light, but had a lot of batter. Just stick to one piece. Overall not bad for $20 but only worth coming if there’s no line.

author: J
aspect_detail: Udon noodles were soft and chewy
original_text: Loved it! Udon noodles were soft and chewy, they were so good. The tempura was also really fresh and delicious. The squid tempura, in particular, was super tender, moist, and flavorful! The sauces were selfserve, which was a bit inconvenient, but being able to choose different ones and dip the toppings actually made it even better.

author: Simon W
aspect_detail: noodles I felt weren't as chewy for freshly made udon
original_text:
Came on day 3 of the soft opening and waited 40 minute from the washroom until the point of payment.

Heard great things about this place and I thought it met expectations. I ordered the large #6 curry udon and loaded up on some tempura. The noodles I felt weren't as chewy for freshly made udon, maybe it was over cooked. The squid tempura was really good, but I wouldn't get the chicken tempura again because it was a bit greasy. Two of my friends got egg tempura and one was overcooked so it wasn't runny unfortunately :(. Service was really good as there was staff helping us scope out a table.

Would recommend and would come back, but when there's a shorter wait.

author: Sarah T
original_text: cheaper and even better than the waikiki location <3

author: Samantha Lee
aspect_detail: the udon here has no chew
original_text:
As someone who loves udon, I don't think this place is worth the wait. I would come back if I happened to be in the area and wanted a quick, relatively affordable meal and there was no line.

I enjoyed the nikutama beef and the shrimp and zucchini tempura! Service is also very fast and friendly once you get to the ordering station.

I found the udon itself to be underwhelming. I love udon with a good chew, but the udon here has no chew. Honestly I could find frozen udon at the grocery store with more chew 😅 I only tried the curry and at first it was good, but the taste seemed to get saltier the more I ate. The chicken katsu wasn't great--my friend found that it was salty and tasted slightly burnt. It's also not a delicately crispy katsu, it's more breaded and crunchy (unsure if it was overcooked or if that's the norm).

author: Hien Nguyen
original_text: Cafeteria-style dining experience. Food was great; no complaints.

author: MA R
original_text: I came on the second day after the grand opening. Wait was probably around half hour around 5pm. You order your udon at the counter, sides and drinks are self serve and you pay at the end and seat yourselves. The udon is highly recommended!!! It’s comparable to the ones in Japan. Had the best seller nikutama udon and the chicken katsu curry udon. The shrimp tempura and kaarage were just alright. The tempura sauce was a little watered down which would have made the shrimp tempura. I had this clementine drink was unique. Will definitely be back for more!

author: Mirthful Eastern
original_text:
This is meant to be a quick meal type deal, exactly as advertised. No frills, no fuss, get in get out. It's super crowded now because it's a well known franchise finally coming to Toronto; come back later if you don't get it and hate the crowd, and give the rest of us fans some space back.

PS: Don't load up on tempura, that's where they get ya. 1 is enough, 2 as a treat.

This is meant to be a quick meal type deal, exactly as advertised. No frills, no fuss, get in get out. It's super crowded now because it's a well known franchise finally coming to Toronto; come back later if you don't get it and hate the crowd, and give the rest of us fans some space back.

PS: Don't load up on tempura, that's where they get ya. 1 is enough, 2 as a treat.

author: Paul Murata
original_text:
Not bad at all. The ingredients are fresh and the place brings back memories of Japan. It's still very new so it gets pretty crowded at times but the line goes fairly quickly and people eating don't stay too long so there's a good flow.
Udon is great and tempura extras are good. Goyza was "ma-ma" but everything else was good.
Had to wait a bit for curry to be ready (surprised since we just got there when it opened so why wasnt it ready?) But, otherwise, all went okay.

author: DFX
original_text:
An affordable cafeteria style sit down restaurant with udon and tempura dishes. There are not many options when it comes to healthier sides or vegetables.

Overall, it can be a great affordable lunch spot if you are around the area, but personally it was not worth lining up for or going out of your way to visit.

author: Candy Choi
aspect_detail: noodles have a light chew and bounce
original_text:
A solid bang for your buck, no frills udon spot that brings a new Japanese restaurant to the neighborhood. The broth is flavorful and comfortable, and the noodles have a light chew and bounce. There's lots of tempura and sides to choose from, as well as a small drink selection of both Western and Japanese offerings.

Going on Monday right at 12pm, the wait in line was about 20 minutes and moved quickly. Finding seating isn't too difficult as turnover is fast. Make sure to remember to bring your tray and clear your table before you leave. I was in and out within the hour.

author: Nat
aspect_detail: The food we had overall was good but nothing outstanding
original_text: very excited to try this restaurant. The food we had overall was good but nothing outstanding, can't argue since the price point is great and service was super quick and friendly despite the long line. The lineup inside is a but confusing though as they take up space and people just try to reserve seats even though they're so far along in the line.

author: Andy Ly
original_text:
I visited for the soft opening near the end of the night, and it was quite an event. When we walked in, we were told they were almost out of udon, but we luckily snagged one of the very last portions in line.

I ordered the Chicken Katsu Curry Udon. Since there was no Katsu ready, the staff held my noodles aside while I paid so they could bring the full bowl out once the chicken was ready. Unfortunately, there was some confusion, and they accidentally gave my reserved noodles to someone else. By the time the mistake was realized, they were completely sold out.

While I was sad to miss out on the udon I’d been looking forward to, I find that the staff handled the situation well. To compensate for the mix-up, they refunded my original udon, gave me a Katsu Curry Rice for free, and let me have as much tempura as I wanted.

Some extra feedback, the tempura station labels of food were really mixed up and some did not properly represent what was behind the sign so it got confusing what I was looking at. Again, I’m sure this will be improved as tike comes.

My girlfriend enjoyed her udon, and the curry rice was great. The tempura was decent, too. I think the food it’s solid and fair for its price.

It’s clear they’re still finding their rhythm during the soft opening, but I can definitely see the passion and willingness to get things right for the customers. I’ll definitely be back to finally try the udon!

author: Esther So Yeon Lee
original_text:
Overall best Japanese Udon I’ve had in Downtown Toronto!

Everything was freshly made, food tasted really good and was well seasoned. Staff seemed very well coordinated despite the long line from dinner/opening rush. I’ve had better udon in Markham but in DT i would say this is the best.

I was surprised by how good the tempura is, Chikawa and Croquette were very good. Price is good, making this budget friendly but doesn’t loose quality or taste. Will return!

author: Alan Lam
original_text: Marugame Udon is known for making their udon fresh in-house, and that’s a big part of the appeal. You can actually watch them smashing the dough, cutting the noodles, and boiling everything fresh right in front of you. They even measure each bowl precisely, so there’s a real sense of consistency in what you’re getting. I tried the Kake Udon in a large size, which is their classic fish stock soup, and the broth was really tastey: savoury, comforting, with just the right amount of richness, fat, and saltiness without feeling too heavy. The noodles were also spot on, with that proper chewy texture you want from fresh udon. I added the Chicken Katsu and Shrimp Tempura on the side. The Katsu was actually really good, and the portion size felt worth the price. That said, once you start adding sides and a drink, it adds up fast, my total came to $28 after tax with just a bottle of water. If you stick to just the udon and maybe one extra, you can probably keep it closer to around $20. I got the large, but honestly if you have a smaller appetite, the small is probably more than enough. I liked the fast, efficient vibe, but the place is also well designed with lots of seating, so it still feels comfortable and not too hectic. Overall, solid spot if you want fresh udon in a casual setting that still feels nice, just know the add-ons can push the bill up pretty quickly.

I tried the Kake Udon in a large size, which is their classic fish stock soup, and the broth was really tastey: savoury, comforting, with just the right amount of richness, fat, and saltiness without feeling too heavy. The noodles were also spot on, with that proper chewy texture you want from fresh udon. I added the Chicken Katsu and Shrimp Tempura on the side. The Katsu was actually really good, and the portion size felt worth the price. That said, once you start adding sides and a drink, it adds up fast, my total came to $28 after tax with just a bottle of water. If you stick to just the udon and maybe one extra, you can probably keep it closer to around $20. I got the large, but honestly if you have a smaller appetite, the small is probably more than enough. I liked the fast, efficient vibe, but the place is also well designed with lots of seating, so it still feels comfortable and not too hectic. Overall, solid spot if you want fresh udon in a casual setting that still feels nice, just know the add-ons can push the bill up pretty quickly.

author: 태양
original_text:
It’s alright.

This location is pretty consistent with the others I’ve been to (it’s a chain restaurant).

But quite honestly, I’m not a huge fan of the noodle texture. It’s chewy, but in a more sticky way than bouncy chewy. Some of the tempura is better than others (shrimp is good, egg was overcooked) but the turnover is fast so it’s fresh. The matcha was really wasted down as well.

Since it’s priced decently, has a clean layout and simple to order menu, it’s easy to see why it’s so busy.

Rating probably closer to 3.5

author: Moon Kim
aspect_detail: noodles despite being made from scratch was not chewy
original_text: We waited 30 minutes but the food was really disappointing. The noodles despite being made from scratch was not chewy. I don't know if it was sitting out too long or overcooked. The tempura was also very soggy and my zucchini tempura batter was peeling off of it and wet. The soup and sauce is okay, nothing special and not that great. I felt nauseous by of the grease by the end despite only having two tempura. The automatic door opening button was also broken.

author: Carina Chan
aspect_detail: udon to be dense and not chewy
original_text:
I was very excited that Marugame opened up in Toronto. The only time I’ve tried this restaurant was in Waikiki. The portions are big for the price. In Hawaii, there are options for cold/hot broth as well.

The reason for the 3 stars is that I found the udon to be dense and not chewy despite being freshly made.  So that is a big disappointment to me.

The seating is also a bit tight and can be difficult to navigate in/out of your table.

Overall, for the portion you get, it’s a decent value.

author: Its F38s
original_text:
Super excited that this finally opened in Toronto. Came here to enjoy a bowl of udon and was not disappointed. The soup was flavourful and the accompanying tempura was also delicious. I like the concept of selecting your own meal set before paying and sitting down. We choose one soup and one sauce based and both tasted good. I’ve had this chain in Japan before so it was very nostalgic.

As it was new it was quite packed but overall the line flowed well and moved quickly. Well organized. I wished the noodles were chewier in texture a bit more but otherwise it was good.

author: Emily Liu
original_text: Pretty good! Got the #6 and I felt like the curry and the beef were tasty. For the price I would also say this was well worth it. The line moves pretty fast, we went at 4:30 ish and got in within 15 mins. The udon is freshly made and it was chewy and quite delicious. The day we went though they were short on noodles (a bit interesting for a noodle restaurant) so they only served regular size and no large sizes.

The day we went though they were short on noodles (a bit interesting for a noodle restaurant) so they only served regular size and no large sizes.

author: Yijae Chang Chien
original_text: We had been waiting for almost a year for the opening! Even it was a bit crowded during grand opening and the waiting time was 30 mins, the food and experience still outperformed! You cannot get a cheaper but good udon elsewhere in Toronto. I would recommend to go with a regular udon and order more tempuras as sides so you don’t get too full. Would definitely come back to try other tempuras in near future.

author: Jane Tam
original_text: We got the Beef Udon and the Nikutama Udon. Both were really tasty. I love the cafeteria style layout and being able to see the staff make the udon fresh. There's a self serve tempura and drinks section with a wide selection. Prices are pretty reasonable and I was impressed that there was no tipping prompt when we went to pay. The place is super clean with lots of seating. Excited to come back!

author: Samuel S
aspect_detail: Udon was great. Great bounce and absorbed all the flavours in the soup
original_text:
Udon was great. Great bounce and absorbed all the flavours in the soup. Knocking down one star because the tempura was pretty bad. Used to a light and airy batter with my tempura, but the one we got today was thicker and not too flavourful even with the dipping sauce.

Love the fast food cafeteria style concept. It was great trying it in Japan and loved that they brought it to Canada.

author: Stanley C
aspect_detail: udon texture is not as chewy
original_text: First time I had this udon was the one in Hawaii about 13 years ago, loved it; have been waiting to see this in Toronto and it’s finally here. I don’t eat udon except for this restaurant. Drove all the way to downtown just for this. The taste is the same, however the udon texture is not as chewy. Soup base is good. The shrimp tempura is wonderful. The decoration and business model are pretty much the same as Hawaii. The staffs are friendly and welcoming. Enough seats and didn’t have to wait long. Self serve cold water, sauces and utensils. Price is very reasonable for the udon. They use hot water to warm up and clean the bowl before putting the udon in! Nice and clean washrooms. Hope they will open more in the Toronto area.

author: Fiona
original_text:
I visited Margame Udon twice even before the grand opening, and I already fell in love with it!

The udon texture is amazing - perfectly chewy and fresh. The shrimp tempura is honestly the best - super crispy and so satisfying. The soup is also very comforting. I always leave feeling so happy after the meal. I’ve even recommended it to my colleagues, and they all loved it too!

I already loved this brand when I had it in Japan, so I was really excited to see it here.

Just a small suggestion - the return area can get a bit messy, and sometimes you have to wait for staff to collect trays before you can leave. Also, tables could be cleaned a bit more frequently, especially during busy times when there are long lines. It might help to have more staff focusing on cleaning.

Overall, still a great experience and I’ll definitely keep coming back!
- ̗̀ ( ˶'ᵕ'˶) ̖́-

author: Alan Siu Kuen Wong
original_text:
Honestly, the UDon is good; but the soup and the Donkatsu are leabing to the salty side. I have a curry chicken. The chicken is over done and dry. Overall it is fine; but not extraordinary. My wife has a beef UDon. It is ok. The surprise is their deep fried shrimp and zucchini. The outside is crunchy and the inside is still juicy These two are great.
The line is long but it is not a long wait because the turnover is very fast.

My wife has a beef UDon. It is ok. The surprise is their deep fried shrimp and zucchini. The outside is crunchy and the inside is still juicy These two are great.
The line is long but it is not a long wait because the turnover is very fast.

author: Fare C (FAREvolution)
original_text: Experience is tilting.  This place isn’t a restaurant, more like a cafeteria.  People just steal and hold tables as soon as they walk in.  There you are standing with a tray of food looking for a seat after waiting in line for 15 mins or so.  Hopefully, they manage this problem soon.

author: Michelle Eats
original_text:
Visited one day after soft opening and was trying to look for line up photos/video but didn't see much.

Plot twist there is a line and it's inside! A plus because it was really cold in Toronto.

From joining the line, ordering and getting our food, took -/+30mins!! I was glad the line moved quickly.

The concept is like ikea lol, order, pay, and grab a seat. The tempura, drinks, sauces, water, utensils is self serve.

The staff members are constantly cleaning tables but help customers find empty tables.

Affordable pricing compare to other udon joints. Would definitely return and try other dishes.

I got the kake and BK udon regular size, the kake is soupy and light, whereas the BK is more flavourful and kinda of gave dipping ramen.

For the tempura we got, shrimp, pumpkin, chicken, zucchini and karaage. Skip on the chicken tempura and just got the karaage, more flavourful and tender.

author: Sarah Yoon
original_text:
The prices are affordable, the portions are generous, and everything tasted great.
I really liked that they make the udon noodles fresh by hand, and you can even watch the whole process.🍜
The tempura on the side was super crispy and delicious, especially the shrimp tempura.
If you order udon, definitely try the shrimp tempura!🍤
There are plenty of seats inside as well.
The line looked really long, but I was able to order in about 20mins

author: Anni Zhou
original_text:
Yum! Super reasonably priced (their original Kake udon is $6.49 for a reg), good texture of noodle, and the line moves quickly because it’s cafeteria style. From the tempura - shrimp one was best. I do wish the broth was hotter and the seats are kind of hard to maneuver around when they’re all pushed out (hard to walk between tables).

Overall solid spot for a decently priced hot meal in Toronto. I would definitely come back to try the other options!

author: Tiffany Ngan
original_text:
Been waiting FOREVER for a Marugame close to home since I first tried them in Hawaii! So glad they are now open in Toronto!

Everything about this location is consistent and on par with their other locations (Vancouver, LA). The wait during first day of soft opening was 20 mins, and immediately upon ordering the food, seats had opened up (quick, turnkey)!

I ordered my favourite Kitsune Udon (regular $9.49), noodles were hot and chewy, broth was just right, the deep fried tofu was sweet and flavourful— nicely complemented with the green onions and tempura bits. There is a wide variety of fried tempuras to choose from, but the you always have to get the shrimp as it’s the best addition to the noodles!

I can’t wait to go back again for the other menu items, you can’t go wrong when it comes to Marugame!

author: Kevin Cox
original_text:
Good udon and great tempura. My only minor complaint is that all of the soup options are on the sweeter side which I don't prefer. But I think that is authentic to the style.

Also love that they don't ask for a tip. Makes checkout so nice and quick.

Good udon and great tempura. My only minor complaint is that all of the soup options are on the sweeter side which I don't prefer. But I think that is authentic to the style.

Also love that they don't ask for a tip. Makes checkout so nice and quick.

author: David L
aspect_detail: The udon itself is standard—nothing wrong, but nothing memorable either
original_text:
Tried Marugame Udon during soft opening. Overall, it’s decent—but not something I’d go out of my way for.

The setup is very cafeteria-style: you move down the line, pick your udon, then add tempura and sides. Efficient, but not exactly a “sit-down” experience.

Food:
•	The udon itself is standard—nothing wrong, but nothing memorable either.
•	The curry leaned more floral and had a thick, starchy consistency rather than a rich, saucy texture I was expecting.
•	Portioning was a bit disappointing. Even ordering a large, you mostly just get more noodles—not much extra meat. The pork and beef portions felt pretty skimpy.
•	Chicken karaage was a letdown—served cold and cut into small pieces.
•	The standout was definitely the tempura: light, crispy, and a solid variety. Easily the best part of the meal.

Value:
Paid about $50 for two without drinks, which feels steep for what you get. Having been to Japan multiple times, this is easily 2x the price for a lower overall quality experience.

Final thoughts:
It’s convenient, the location is great, and it scratches the itch if you’re craving quick Japanese comfort food—but expectations should be tempered. There’s potential, especially if they fix consistency and portioning.

author: Maggie Chu
original_text:
Came at 4:45 for the soft opening and seems like half of Toronto also had the same idea cuz it was PACKED. Despite that the Marugame team had the line moving with QUICKNESS.
Food was stellar. Had the beef udon and its dipping sauce counterpart. Staff super friendly. Quality of tempura sooo delish with the crunch. It’s giving I’m back in a udon spot in Japan, hello!

author: bv
aspect_detail: if you just get the udon
original_text:
Omg i was super excited for this place to open!! I honestly cant believe how affordable this place is if you just get the udon!! ♪(๑ᴖ◡ᴖ๑)♪ we got the nikutama udon and the kitsune udon!! The BK sauces ones are pretty salty DO NOT DRINK THE SOUP LOL!!

Everyone pleasee try the chicken or shrimp tempura!!!! ʕ•ᴥ•ʔi swear its the best most crunchiest but also juicy tender tempura ive had!! If you’re ballin also the squid was super amazing… i honestly think the quality of the tempura is worth the wait and also the additional price ʕ•ᴥ•ʔ

author: Thanuja V
original_text: This popular udon chain is now in downtown Toronto. They offer udon made fresh daily along with don as well. Thr udon is perfectly chewy noodles, and the menu features rich, comforting flavour pairings.  I really enjoyed the chicken katsu udon option.  Simple, authentic, and so satisfying onna cold day.  They even have a tempura station and sauce station too on the other side of the restaurant. Make sure to check it out. The perfect comfort food!

author: Michelle Wang
original_text:
Came here for the soft opening. Once service started, we were able to get our food and seats very quickly. Lots of staff working at each station, and greeted everyone as they came up to order their bowl. Tempura is self served, pick up what you want with tongs.
After you pay, they have a self-serve station for utensils, napkins, filtered water, and dipping sauce for your food.

We got beef udon, nikutama udon, chicken karaage, shrimp & chicken tempura. The kake broth is flavourful and umami rich, subtle sweet but not too much. The beef added on top contributed the most to the sweet flavour. I would probably just have the kake udon next time - personal preference. The onsen egg was delicious, perfectly silky and coats the beef and udon noodles just right. Tempura were fresh and added positive notes to the experience.

Will definitely come back and try their other menu options

original_text:
Came here for the soft opening. Once service started, we were able to get our food and seats very quickly. Lots of staff working at each station, and greeted everyone as they came up to order their bowl. Tempura is self served, pick up what you want with tongs.
After you pay, they have a self-serve station for utensils, napkins, filtered water, and dipping sauce for your food.

We got beef udon, nikutama udon, chicken karaage, shrimp & chicken tempura. The kake broth is flavourful and umami rich, subtle sweet but not too much. The beef added on top contributed the most to the sweet flavour. I would probably just have the kake udon next time - personal preference. The onsen egg was delicious, perfectly silky and coats the beef and udon noodles just right. Tempura were fresh and added positive notes to the experience.

Will definitely come back and try their other menu options

author: Charlie C
original_text:
Excite for this one! I tried the one in LA and BGC in Metro Manila and it was good! I did go to Japan but did not try the one there, preferred the non chain and non-fast food restaurants!

I just saw this yesterday since I was planning to got to Japan again and then Toronto location popped up in my google search!

I will update REVIEW after I tried the store herein Toronto!

author: nickel
original_text: The noodles are very yummy. I really like the broth in the beef udon. Good price also. However, the restaurant is more food-court style and the seats don’t have backs. You have to get up to get everything and the seating is crowded and not designed for you to stay very long. If you’re just looking for a quick bite though this is a good spot
